summary: Audit every markdown doc against the actual repo state (M0-M2 built, V2.2 applied) and flag staleness

reports.cluster: LIVE-STATE / working docs (memory/project-status.md, memory/working-agreements.md, CLAUDE.md, README.md, docs/README.md)

discrepancies.issue: The whole doc still frames the build as not-yet-started and the docs-explosion as a forward plan whose precondition is unmet: "Build M0+M1 against the *current* `prd.md` (the single source above), then reorganise **once**, later, on ground that has survived play. When the split happens (post-M0/M1 playtest) it freezes...". Ground truth: M0+M1+M2 are BUILT, verify-green (51 tests), and play-tested (overnight build session, commits 8bf6ac9 / 248bf93 / playtest-fix fc36172). The M0/M1 'first, then reorganise' precondition is already satisfied; only the explosion itself is still pending (no docs/roadmap.md, no design/ yet). A cold reader of this index would conclude no game code exists.
discrepancies.suggestedFix: Update the framing to present tense: 'M0–M2 are built and play-tested; the docs-explosion remains deferred (decision pending) even though its M0/M1-playtest precondition is now met.' Keep the D-021 rationale, but stop implying the build hasn't started.

discrepancies.issue: Stale banner example: the recommended roadmap.md banner reads *"M0–M1 committed; M2–M7 provisional, re-planned after each playtest"*. M2 (a+b combat) is now also committed (commits 248bf93, fc36172).
discrepancies.suggestedFix: Change the example banner to '"M0–M2 committed; M3–M7 provisional, re-planned after each playtest".'

discrepancies.issue: docs/content/ is described as a future artifact — "the §4 balance numbers become **generated** `content/` tables (re-tuned after each playtest)" — but docs/content/ already EXISTS (docs/content/t0-content.md, generated by scripts/gen-docs.ts and checked by `npm run verify`'s gen:docs --check). The doc also inventories docs/ as only prd.md + history/ (+ future design/), omitting the now-present living docs content/, fun-factor.md, ui-design.md, prd_human_feedback.md, and plans/.
discrepancies.suggestedFix: State that docs/content/ exists now (e.g. t0-content.md is generated) and add the present living docs (fun-factor.md, ui-design.md, prd_human_feedback.md, plans/) to the docs/ inventory.

discrepancies.issue: Layout section, docs/ bullet: "Generated content/balance tables will land under `docs/content/`." Future tense for something that already exists — docs/content/t0-content.md is generated by scripts/gen-docs.ts and verify-content.ts reports the registries (30 surfaces, 4 activities, 4 ranks, 5 mobs, 2 weapons, 16 names).
discrepancies.suggestedFix: Change to present: 'Generated content/balance tables live under `docs/content/` (e.g. t0-content.md), produced by scripts/gen-docs.ts.'

discrepancies.issue: Freeze bullet: "Build M0+M1 against the current [prd.md](docs/prd.md) **first**, playtest, **then** explode it — freeze §1 as a tagged vision snapshot, move the roadmap to a **living** `docs/roadmap.md`, and **generate** balance into `docs/content/`." The 'Build M0+M1 first, playtest' half is already done (M0–M2 built & play-tested) and balance is already partly generated into docs/content/; only the freeze/explode + docs/roadmap.md remain undone. Reads as wholly-pending forward work.
discrepancies.suggestedFix: Note the precondition is now met: 'M0–M2 are built & play-tested and docs/content/ exists; the freeze of §1 + the move to a living docs/roadmap.md is the pending next step.'

discrepancies.issue: Build-size figures are understated/stale: "`npm run build` (→ `dist/`, ~27 KB JS gz 10 KB + 11 KB CSS — itch-ready, relative-base)". The committed dist build is now JS raw ~42 KB / gz ~15 KB and CSS raw ~13.6 KB / gz ~3.7 KB (it grew with the M2 + polish work). Everything else in this snapshot verified accurate.
discrepancies.suggestedFix: Refresh to the current build: '~42 KB JS (gz ~15 KB) + ~14 KB CSS (gz ~4 KB)'.

discrepancies.issue: "Once a toolchain exists, also gate on its verify command and record it in [project-status](project-status.md)." The toolchain now exists and `npm run verify` (tsc + eslint + prettier + vitest + verify-content + gen:docs --check) is recorded in project-status and passes. The 'Once a toolchain exists' conditional is satisfied but still phrased as anticipatory.
discrepancies.suggestedFix: Restate as a standing rule in present tense: 'Gate every commit on `npm run verify` (recorded in project-status).'

discrepancies.issue: "durable design in [`../docs/`](../docs/) (added when we design the game)" — the parenthetical implies docs are not yet authored, but docs/ is now richly populated (prd.md, prd_human_feedback.md, fun-factor.md, ui-design.md, plans/, content/, history/).
discrepancies.suggestedFix: Drop or update the parenthetical, e.g. 'durable design in ../docs/ (living docs, edited in place).'

discrepancies.issue: Root README (designated by CLAUDE.md as 'the game's vision') is still the original raw human seed prompt and contradicts evolved canon: "You start out as a 17 year old farmhand" whereas the canonical age is ~18–20 (docs/prd.md §1; project-status.md says '~18yo'). It also still reads as a pre-build brief — "Feel free to use HTML5 & Typescript in the implementation", "For our story I will seed you a hint", "look at proto23" — with none of the now-canonical specifics (title Kamikakushi, true name Tahei, the Kurosawa house, 5 tiers / House Influence) that live in docs/prd.md §1.
discrepancies.suggestedFix: Either fix the age (17 → ~18) and add a one-line pointer to docs/prd.md §1 as the authoritative vision, or explicitly label this file the preserved original seed so the 17yo/seed framing isn't read as current canon.

accurate: memory/project-status.md milestone state is correct: 'PRD V2.2 DONE → DEMO BUILT (M0+M1+M2) & POLISHED' — matches commits 2b8d5e9 (V2.2), 8bf6ac9 (M0+M1), 248bf93 (M2 a+b), plus polish/playtest commits 45828b6/fc36172/ca3eef0.
Test count is exact and current: project-status claims '51 unit tests green' — verified `npx vitest run` = 51 passed across 7 files (scaffold 3, rng 9, format 5, engine 8, m1 7, m2 10, save 9).

'`npm run verify` passes' is true — ran the full pipeline (tsc + eslint + prettier + vitest + verify-content + gen:docs --check); exit 0, prettier clean, verify-content OK (30 surfaces/4 activities/4 ranks/5 mobs/2 weapons/16 names), gen:docs up to date.

The toolchain exists exactly as described: package.json has dev/build/build:itch/test/lint/format/gen:docs/verify scripts; Vite 5 + TS strict + Vitest 2 + ESLint 9 flat + Prettier; vite.config.ts, tsconfig.json, eslint.config.js all present.

The src/ layout in project-status is real and matches: src/core (rng, state, step, intents, unlock, rewards, log, skills, ranks, combat, fight, selectors, format, content/* registries), src/persistence (saveManager/backends/codec/migrate/validate), src/ui (render.ts + styles.css), src/app/main.ts; scripts/{verify-content,gen-docs}.ts both present.
docs/content/ exists with generated t0-content.md (refutes any 'will land under docs/content' framing being still-future for project-status, which correctly treats it as present).

Canonical NPC/house names match code: src/core/content/names.ts has lord Shigemasa, drillmaster Kihei, physician Sōan, house Kurosawa, trueName Tahei — and the comments document the Yagyū/Edogawa-echo renames (Q39/Block N). project-status references Sōan and Kurosawa consistently.
ADRs D-044 (crash-recovery: error boundary + last-known-good ring + safe-mode boot) and D-045 (a11y-ink rule) are present in docs/history/decisions.md, as project-status claims.

How-to-resume is valid: the referenced journal/2026-06-26-session-02-overnight-build.md exists; tmp/qa-shots.mjs exists; the Next step (M3: T0 R4→R7) is genuinely forward and M3–M7 / T3–T4 remain correctly roadmap-only.
CLAUDE.md Layout otherwise matches reality: scripts/snapshot-research.sh, brainstorms/raw/, audit/, tmp/, human-in-the-loop/, journal/_TEMPLATE.md all exist as described.
reports.notes: memory/project-status.md is the live snapshot and is essentially accurate and current — the only stale datum is the build-size line (the bundle grew with M2/polish). The staleness is concentrated in the two 'plan/intent' docs that were written pre-build and not refreshed: docs/README.md (worst — it still narrates 'Build M0+M1 first... when the split happens post-M0/M1 playtest', as if no code exists, and treats docs/content/ as future) and, more lightly, CLAUDE.md (one 'will land under docs/content/' future-tense + the 'Build M0+M1 first, playtest, then explode' freeze bullet whose precondition is now met). working-agreements.md has two anticipatory conditionals ('once a toolchain exists', 'added when we design the game') that already resolved. Root README.md is the un-evolved original human seed (17yo vs canon ~18–20). No blockers found and nothing falsely claims the build is broken; all severities are minor except the docs/README.md build-state mischaracterization (major). Note: the docs-explosion / docs/roadmap.md genuinely has NOT happened yet (no docs/roadmap.md, no design/), so the 'deferred' status itself is still literally true — what's stale is the language implying its M0/M1-playtest trigger is still in the future. One out-of-scope observation: src/ui/styles.css shows as modified in the working tree, but verify is still green.

reports.cluster: docs/prd.md — internal-consistency audit after V2.2 (Block N) application

discrepancies.issue: Header status/provenance line (line 3) is one version behind: "**Status: PRD V2 — reshaped from the 79 human-signed V2 decisions** (Block L `Q1–Q56` + Block M `FU1–FU23`, 2026-06-26...". The body now incorporates the 32 Block N + 7 N.1 (V2.1) decisions — 140 in-body `D-Q*`/`Block N` references — and was committed as "PRD V2.2" (commit 2b8d5e9); project-status.md calls the current spec "PRD V2.2 DONE". The status line names only Block L+M and omits Block N / N.1 / V2.1 / V2.2 entirely. The §-status table (lines 21-29) likewise tags every section "**PRD V2**".
discrepancies.suggestedFix: Relabel the status line to "PRD V2.2 — Block L Q1–Q56 + Block M FU1–FU23 + Block N (32 post-battery, D-043) + Block N.1 (7); ADRs D-044/D-045 added" and bump the date; update the §-status table cells from "PRD V2" to "PRD V2.2".

discrepancies.issue: The code-gate claim is now factually false. Header "How to read" block (lines 7-8): "No code is written until §7 (the roadmap) is approved." and §7 intro (line 6845): "This is the **last PRD section** and the one that, once approved, unlocks code (... *no code is written until §7 is approved*)." In reality M0+M1+M2a+M2b are built, committed (8bf6ac9, 248bf93) and verify-green (51 unit tests; project-status.md), so §7 is effectively approved and in execution.
discrepancies.suggestedFix: Replace both with a past-tense status note, e.g. "§7 was approved 2026-06-26; M0/M1/M2a/M2b are SHIPPED & verify-green (51 tests); M3–M7 remain provisional."

discrepancies.issue: §7.2 milestone roadmap reads as entirely forward-looking with NO completion view. The M0 (line 7046), M1 (7102), M2a (7143) and M2b (7189) entries are written in pure spec/future tense ("**Goal:** stand up...", "**Lands:**", "**Definition of done:** verify green; ...") with no DONE/SHIPPED marker, even though all four are complete (project-status.md: "DEMO BUILT (M0+M1+M2)... 51 unit tests green"). A cold reader of this 'what-it-is-now' doc cannot tell M0–M2b are built. (The planned migration of §7 to a living docs/roadmap.md has not happened — roadmap.md is absent.)
discrepancies.suggestedFix: Add a per-milestone status tag to M0/M1/M2a/M2b (e.g. "**Status: SHIPPED — commit 8bf6ac9/248bf93**") or a §7.2 banner: "M0–M2b complete & verify-green; M3a onward forward/provisional."

discrepancies.issue: Forward-looking reorg guidance overtaken by events. The framing block (lines 41-45): "**Do NOT explode this doc yet** — **M0 + M1 build against this PRD as-is**; full sign-off and the one-time reorganisation come **after the first build-and-play cycle** ... §7 moves to a living `docs/roadmap.md` ... the §4 numbers move to generated `docs/content/` tables." M0/M1 (and M2) are built AND playtested (playtest-fix commit fc36172), so 'the first build-and-play cycle' has occurred and the promised reorg is now the pending next step — yet roadmap.md and docs/balance/ still don't exist, and the imperative reads as not-yet-started.
discrepancies.suggestedFix: Annotate that M0–M2b are built/playtested and the one-time reorg (freeze §1 snapshot, §7→docs/roadmap.md, §4→docs/content/balance tables) is now the queued next step rather than a future trigger.

discrepancies.issue: §1.10 "V2 deltas" ADR roster is stale (line 1057): "The V2 reshape adds ADRs **D-016 … D-042** (the 56 Block-L decisions + 23 Block-M follow-ups → the new D-023…D-042 set)." decisions.md now also carries D-043 (PRD V2.1 post-battery), D-044 (crash-recovery), D-045 (a11y-ink). The PRD never cites D-043/D-044/D-045 by number (grep empty) even though their substance IS woven in under Block N decision tags — §6.8.3 crash-recovery is tagged `D-Q-B12`, the a11y ink rule §2.16 is tagged `D-Q-a11y` and line 2434 even says "a new UI/a11y ADR" without the number.
discrepancies.suggestedFix: Extend the range to "D-016 … D-045" and add a clause: "+ Block N → D-043 (V2.1), D-044 (crash-recovery), D-045 (a11y-ink)"; back-fill the D-044/D-045 numbers at §6.8.3 and §2.16 where they currently say only D-Q-B12 / D-Q-a11y / 'a new UI/a11y ADR'.

discrepancies.issue: Village-chief name PRD-vs-code mismatch (audit item 5). The PRD uses "Yagōemon" (with macron) universally — incl. as the macron-lint's own POSITIVE example at §6.6 (line 6343: "...Yagōemon, Jūbei...") and §1.10 (line 1109) — but the shipped code string is `villageChief: 'Yagoemon'` (plain ASCII, no macron), which the PRD's own macron/no-plain-ASCII-romaji lint says must be flagged. The three Block-N renames (Shigemasa/Kihei/Sōan) DO match code; this one strand drifted.
discrepancies.suggestedFix: Change names.ts `villageChief` to 'Yagōemon' to match the PRD and satisfy its macron-lint (or, if 'Yagoemon' is the intended Hepburn, correct every PRD occurrence + the lint example instead).

discrepancies.issue: Two minor residue items from the Block N renames. (a) The macron-lint POSITIVE-example list (line 6343) still cites "Jūbei", a character name retired by Block N (now Kihei) — using a removed name as the canonical macron exemplar is stale (this is the only non-annotation use of an old name, and it's a lint example, not live prose). (b) The real-name DENYLIST's superseded-names guard (lines 6400-6401) flags Mago / Naozane / Obaa Sato so they can't be reintroduced, but does NOT include the Block-N-retired Munenori / Jūbei / Ranpo, so by the same logic those renamed-away names lack the reintroduction guard.
discrepancies.suggestedFix: Swap the line-6343 lint example from "Jūbei" to a live macron name (e.g. Kyūsuke or Sōzaemon); add Munenori/Jūbei/Ranpo to the §6.6.1 superseded-name denylist alongside Mago/Naozane/Obaa Sato.

accurate: T2 revealed-pillar COUNT is consistently 4 (Arms + Estate + Office + Name). The only two '3–4' hits (lines 6277, 6391) are explicit corrections — both read "...T2 = 4 (+ Name ... NOT '3–4')" — so no stale '3-4 pillars' claim survives; §1.6.4/§4/§6 all say T0=2 / T1=3 / T2=4.

No ACTIVE prose names a live character with an old name. Jūbei/Ranpo/Munenori appear only in the to-be-KEPT "renamed off the Yagyū/Edogawa X echo" annotations (lines 714/720/724/1028-1030/5877-5878) and the rename-mapping tables (4928-4929) — the live names everywhere are Kihei / Sōan / Shigemasa.

Canonical NPC names match the code. §1.8 cast (lord Shigemasa 714, drillmaster Kihei 720, physician Sōan 724) and §6 (5877-5878) tie out with src/core/content/names.ts (lord 'Shigemasa', drillmaster 'Kihei', physician 'Sōan'), plus heir Naoyuki / elder Genemon / steward Chiyo / Sayo / Jinpachi / Tahei / Tama / Otsuru.

Schema item: pendingAppraisals is a COUNTER, not a boolean — explicitly contrasted with the retired `pendingAppraisalDue` boolean at lines 1256, 6136, 6204-6205, 6405; the tick scheduler drains it one-per-count so a multi-season jump accrues all N appraisals.

Schema item: quests are ORDER-FREE — `QuestStatus = 'taken'|'active'|'abandoned'|'done'|'failed'` with an unordered `advancedBy` Set and explicitly "NO `step` cursor, no fixed order" (lines 1818-1819, 6201-6202, 6302, 6518). Consistent throughout.

Schema item: Estate & Wealth value is purely DERIVED, never stored — computed on read as land+treasury+trade so a strand-dent can't desync it and the trade-≤⅓ clamp holds by construction (lines 1332, 6193, 6384, 6515; D-Q-estate-dent). Non-Estate pillars store value; Estate omits it.

Schema item: the deed-only `gateEligibleValue` accumulator is present and consistent (15 occurrences) — Model-A combos credit both pillars' display `value` post-clamp but never write `gateEligibleValue`, and the gate check reads `gateEligibleValue` not the combo-inflated value (lines 6193, 6306, 6331-6333, 6383, 6388).

Schema item: Name is a REAL T2 gate with consistent numbers — 70% good band 19,600 deeds (0.70·28K) + 30% seasonal 8,400 (0.30·28K) = 28K — byte-identical across §4 (lines 3713-3721, 3810, 3851, 3855, 4741, 4744, 4799-4800) and §6 (6276-6277, 6390-6391: "a single value 4 ... NOT '3–4'").

The substance of the new Block N ADRs is present (only their D-044/D-045 numbers are missing): crash-recovery error-boundary + last-known-good ring + safe-mode boot is fully specced at §6.8.3 (D-Q-B12), and the a11y identity-hues-as-fills / meaning-text-in-`--ink-soft` rule at §2.16 (D-Q-a11y).
reports.notes: Verified against repo at HEAD (4f8abc2): src/{core,ui,app,persistence} present, 7 test files / 51 tests, project-status.md confirms \"PRD V2.2 DONE → DEMO BUILT (M0+M1+M2) & POLISHED\". docs/roadmap.md and docs/balance/ are ABSENT (the planned post-build reorg has not run); docs/content/ holds only t0-content.md. The four targeted schema checks (item 3) and the pillar-count (item 1) and live-name-in-prose (item 2) checks are all CLEAN. The real findings cluster around staleness the V2.2 application did not touch: (1) the document still self-identifies as \"PRD V2\" and gates on \"no code until §7 approved\" though M0–M2b shipped, and (2) §7's milestone list has no completion view. Severity calibration: nothing is a blocker; the three majors are factual contradictions with the repo (version label, code-gate claim, all-forward roadmap); the minors are traceability/lint residue. The §7 all-forward finding is partly mitigated because CLAUDE.md assigns live build-status to memory/project-status.md and §7 is slated to migrate to docs/roadmap.md — but as a 'what-it-is-now' doc it currently misreads as nothing-built, so the lightweight fix is a status banner rather than the full reorg. Did NOT flag the to-be-kept \"renamed off the X echo\" annotations per instructions.

reports.cluster: docs/prd_human_feedback.md + docs/history/decisions.md — currency audit (post PRD V2.2 + M0–M2 demo build)

discrepancies.issue: Block N.1 is still marked PENDING implementation: line 589-590 "**Status: 🟡 PROPOSED — confirm at V2.1.** The human may override any of these when reviewing PRD V2.1; until then V2.1 implements the proposed default." Reality: all 7 N.1 defaults were applied to prd.md in PRD V2.2 (commit 2b8d5e9) — e.g. #3 (physician 'Ranpo' → rename) is live as Sōan in prd.md §1.9 (lines 724, 1029-1030, explicitly citing 'Block N.1 #3'). The 🟡 PROPOSED status mis-signals to a cold-pickup agent that these still need confirming/implementing.
discrepancies.suggestedFix: Flip the Block N.1 status banner to '✅ APPLIED in PRD V2.2 (commit 2b8d5e9) — defaults stand unless the human reopens them.' Optionally mark each of the 7 items ✅ (or note any that were superseded), and the closing 'truly-minor items' line as applied.

discrepancies.issue: Block N header still frames the work as a future rewrite: line 520 "They drive the PRD **V2 → V2.1** rewrite." The rewrite is DONE — all 32 Block N decisions were woven into prd.md in PRD V2.2 (commit 2b8d5e9, 943 insertions + audit-fix per memory/project-status.md). The doc nowhere records that V2.2 applied Block N. The whole block reads as queued.
discrepancies.suggestedFix: Add an '✅ APPLIED to docs/prd.md in PRD V2.2 (2b8d5e9)' line under the Block N header. Note that the project landed at V2.2 (commits call it V2.1=decisions-locked, V2.2=applied-to-prd), so the 'V2 → V2.1 rewrite' label is one version behind reality.

discrepancies.issue: The precedence-rule pointer is two blocks stale: line 22 "the **LATEST block** (currently **Block M** — the wave-2 follow-ups — then **Block L**) **supersedes earlier blocks/entries wherever they differ**." Block N (line 515) and Block N.1 (line 586) are now the newest and explicitly self-declare 'AUTHORITATIVE — newest block wins' / 'Block N governs'. An auditor following this line would apply the wrong governing block.
discrepancies.suggestedFix: Update to: 'the LATEST block (currently Block N.1 → Block N → Block M → Block L) supersedes earlier blocks/entries wherever they differ.'

discrepancies.issue: The 'Last updated' footer is orphaned mid-file and stale: line 510 "*Last updated: 2026-06-26. Keep appending as the human steers.*" sits ABOVE Block N (515) and Block N.1 (586), which were appended after it — violating the file's own 'append newest at the bottom' maintenance rule. The date also predates the V2.2 application + the M0–M2 demo build, neither of which is acknowledged anywhere in the doc.
discrepancies.suggestedFix: Move the footer to the true bottom (after line 620), bump the date, and add a one-line note that Blocks N + N.1 were applied to prd.md in PRD V2.2 and the M0–M2 demo is built/verify-green.

discrepancies.issue: Legend omits the 🟡 marker that Block N.1 introduces: line 15 "**Legend:** ✅ implemented · 🔄 in progress · ⏳ queued · 🔁 reversed/superseded." Block N.1 uses 🟡 ('PROPOSED') which is undefined in the legend.
discrepancies.suggestedFix: Add '🟡 proposed (awaiting confirm)' to the legend — or, once Block N.1 is flipped to ✅ APPLIED, the marker becomes moot and no legend change is needed.

discrepancies.issue: Historical open-items in Blocks I/J are still marked pending though long resolved: line 333 "**J7.** §7 milestone breakdown ... — **in progress** (workflow `w46su5inn`)" (PRD V2 §7 is fully authored and M0–M2 are built); line 331-332 "**J6.** Whole-project audit — **pending**" (the 5-round battery + V2/V2.1/V2.2 happened); and line 315 "**I7.** §3.5 / §3.7 — not yet reviewed. ⏳" directly contradicts line 323 "**J3.** §3.5 / §3.7. **DONE** ✅".
discrepancies.suggestedFix: Strike-through/close J6, J7 (mark DONE with the V2/V2.1/V2.2 + battery refs) and reconcile I7's ⏳ with J3's DONE, or add a banner that Blocks I/J are a historical §3-review snapshot superseded by Blocks L–N.

discrepancies.issue: D-021 carries forward-language now contradicted by reality. Decision (line 181): "**DO NOT EXPLODE YET:** build **M0+M1 against the CURRENT `docs/prd.md`**, playtest, **THEN** reorganise once"; Consequences (line 183): "Do NOT create `docs/roadmap.md` or `docs/content/` yet — build M0+M1 against the current `prd.md` first; defer the docs-explosion to the post-M0/M1 playtest." But the first build-and-play cycle has HAPPENED (M0+M1+M2 demo, verify-green, playtested), and `docs/content/t0-content.md` already EXISTS and is GENERATED — so the explosion's trigger has fired AND its content-table half has begun, contradicting 'Do NOT create docs/content/ yet'. The remaining halves (a LIVING `docs/roadmap.md`; a tagged §1 vision snapshot) are NOT done — §7 roadmap still lives in prd.md and prd.md still self-labels 'PRD V2' rather than a frozen vision. The explosion is now partially-done and overdue, and nothing in the ADR log records it.
discrepancies.suggestedFix: Append a dated annotation to D-021 (or add a new ADR D-046): 'first build-play cycle complete (M0/M1/M2 demo, verify-green); docs-explosion trigger has fired and is IN PROGRESS — generated `docs/content/` tables now exist; OUTSTANDING: move §7 roadmap → living `docs/roadmap.md` and tag the §1 vision snapshot.' This also satisfies the question of recording M0–M2 completion in the decision layer.

discrepancies.issue: D-020 Consequences still asserts the pre-D-021 timing: line 176 "Do the docs-explosion **at the start of the build phase**; `gen:docs --check` is part of M0's `npm run verify`; `prd.md` becomes read-mostly once frozen." This is the exact claim D-020's own inline refinement and D-021 overturned ('it is NOT "the first step of the build phase"; explode AFTER the first M0+M1 build-play cycle'). The Decision body was annotated, but the Consequences bullet was left uncorrected, so it still contradicts the governing decision.
discrepancies.suggestedFix: Edit the D-020 Consequences bullet to read 'Do the docs-explosion AFTER the first M0+M1 build-and-play cycle (per the D-021 refinement), not at the start of the build phase' — keeping the gen:docs/read-mostly notes.

accurate: decisions.md D-044 (crash-recovery: error boundary + last-known-good ring + safe-mode + out-of-state crash-counter + autosave-poison suppression) is present and well-formed — full Context/Options/Decision/Why/Consequences, correctly cites Block N item D-Q-B12 and §6.8/M0/M6 consequences.

decisions.md D-045 (a11y ink rule: identity hues = fills/accents only, meaning-bearing text in --ink-soft AA-passing ink, drop the coloured WIN/LOSS word + the 'AA on every surface' overclaim) is present and well-formed, correctly cites Block N item D-Q-a11y and prd §2.21 / ui-design §5.1/§5.3.
The governing-rule chain D-022 → D-043 (PRD V2.1 umbrella) → D-044/D-045 is internally consistent and 'newest-wins' is applied throughout the V2/V2.1 ADR batch (D-023…D-042).

NPC renames are consistent between the docs: Block N D-Q-name-collision + Block N.1 #3 (rename lord/drillmaster off Yagyū Munenori+Jūbei and physician off Edogawa Ranpo) match prd.md's live canon — lord Shigemasa, drillmaster Kihei, physician Sōan — confirming the V2.2 reconcile landed.

decisions.md D-017 + D-021's note that the §7 M2–M7 roadmap is LIVING/provisional and the full-T0–T2 v1 scope lock is UNCHANGED correctly matches reality: M3–M7 are genuinely unbuilt and T3/T4 are roadmap-only.

prd_human_feedback.md Blocks A–M (the vision/constraints, the 56 V2 decisions, the 23 wave-2 follow-ups) remain an accurate intent record; the foundational vision (no-magic, mediocre-start, trade ≤⅓, four pillars, estate spine, ≥30-min floor, 70/30) is honoured by the current prd.md and code.

Block N's decision content itself is substantively accurate vs prd.md V2.2 (e.g. ~60h floor, Name as a real gated pillar, estate builds → Phase 2, Model-A combos, multi-tab unsupported, analytic win-rate, derived Estate value) — the staleness is only in the status framing, not the decisions.
reports.notes:
Scope: I audited the two named files against repo HEAD (git log, src/, docs/, package.json, memory/project-status.md, docs/content/). The substance of both files is sound; the problems are status-marker currency, not wrong decisions.

Headline answers to the brief:
1) prd_human_feedback.md Block N/N.1 are STILL PENDING-FRAMED. Block N says it will 'drive the PRD V2 → V2.1 rewrite' and Block N.1 is '🟡 PROPOSED — confirm at V2.1 ... until then V2.1 implements the proposed default.' Both are stale: PRD V2.2 (commit 2b8d5e9) already wove all Block N + N.1 into prd.md. The doc never records V2.2 / 'woven into prd.md', and the 'Last updated: 2026-06-26' footer is orphaned mid-file (Block N/N.1 were appended after it). The precedence pointer still names 'Block M' as the latest block.
2) decisions.md D-044 and D-045 are present and well-formed. The forward-language problem is in D-020/D-021: they still say 'DO NOT EXPLODE YET' / 'Do NOT create docs/roadmap.md or docs/content/ yet' — but the first M0+M1 build-play cycle has happened (M0/M1/M2 demo, verify-green), so that gate has been passed. D-020's Consequences bullet also still says 'do the docs-explosion at the start of the build phase', which its own refinement overturned.
3) DOCS-EXPLOSION STATUS (the crucial item): it is NOW DUE and PARTIALLY DONE — overdue overall. Done: the GENERATED content-table half has begun (docs/content/t0-content.md exists with a 'GENERATED by npm run gen:docs' header; gen:docs --check is wired into npm run verify). NOT done: docs/roadmap.md is ABSENT (the §7 roadmap still lives inside prd.md), and there is no tagged §1 vision snapshot (prd.md still self-labels 'PRD V2' across its section table, not a frozen vision). So the explosion is ~1/3 complete and the ADR log has NO entry recording either the M0–M2 completion or that the explosion trigger has fired — recommend annotating D-021 (or a new D-046) to that effect.

Out-of-scope cross-reference (not counted as a discrepancy in the two target files, but relevant): docs/prd.md still labels itself 'PRD V2' / 'Status: PRD V2' throughout (line 3 and the §-status table), while the commits/journal call the applied state 'PRD V2.2'. That prd.md version-label lag is the same currency gap surfacing in a third file; worth a follow-up pass on prd.md itself."

reports.cluster: design/plan docs (ui-design.md, fun-factor.md, qa-playtesting.md) vs built M0+M1+M2 demo

discrepancies.issue: Doc-wide status banner (line 3): "> **DRAFT — for human review.**" The renderer is now BUILT to this bible — memory/project-status.md states "docs/ui-design.md (woodblock/ink bible — the renderer is built to it)", and src/ui/render.ts + styles.css implement the woodblock shell, the .frame/hanko/seal system, the §6.2 rank-up seal press, the four-pillar 官威 identities, K/M/B + reduced-motion. The bible is implemented, not an un-reviewed draft.
discrepancies.suggestedFix: Relabel the banner from DRAFT to a LIVING/IMPLEMENTED status, e.g. "Implemented in M1+M2 — the renderer (src/ui) is built to this bible; one open item remains: font loading (Q52)." Keep the genuinely-open font items called out, but stop framing the whole doc as pending first review.

discrepancies.issue: Open choice #1 for the human (lines 620-621): "DRAFT default = **Shippori Mincho** ... + **Yuji Syuku** ... Confirm the pairing before M1." M1 has SHIPPED (commit 8bf6ac9 M0+M1, then M2). The build did NOT get a human confirmation before M1 — it adopted the draft default in the CSS font stack (styles.css --font-head/body/display) but with NO actual font loaded: there is no @font-face and no /public/fonts, so it falls back to system mincho (Hiragino/Yu Mincho). The "before M1" deadline is overtaken by events.
discrepancies.suggestedFix: Update to reflect reality: M1/M2 shipped using the draft Shippori+Yuji stack as the de-facto default WITH a system-mincho fallback (no font self-hosted yet, Q52). Move "confirm the pairing" + actually loading the font from a "before M1" gate to a pre-publish task (Q52 / M6-M7), matching memory/project-status.md "Pre-publish: self-host the OFL font (Q52)".

discrepancies.issue: Open choice #2 for the human (lines 622-624): "**Font delivery:** self-hosted hand-subset `.woff2` (Option B ...) vs Google dynamic subsetting (Option A — zero build step). Recommend **Option B**." This is presented as still-open, but it is RESOLVED: docs/prd.md §6 (Q52 / D-013) locks "self-hosted OFL fonts (kill Google dynamic-subsetting — it breaks offline + the itch relative-base)". The choice is made (Option B), not pending.
discrepancies.suggestedFix: Mark this RESOLVED (like open-choice #4): "Font delivery = self-hosted OFL .woff2 (Option B); Google dynamic-subsetting rejected — Q52/D-013." Remove it from the open-choices list or annotate it as decided.

discrepancies.issue: Status banner (line 7): "> **Status:** PLAN (the harness lands in M0; the loops run from M1 on)." The harness has LANDED — window.__qa is installed in src/app/main.ts (DEV-guarded, line 159; assigned line 283) with state()/dispatch()/pacing()/reveals()/tick()/toRung()/newGame()/save()/load(); M0+M1+M2 are built. The visual/feel loop (§4) has already RUN — audit/ holds desktop+mobile+prod screenshots (qa-01..qa-10, m1-*, prod-*). "PLAN ... lands in M0" reads as future for work already done.
discrepancies.suggestedFix: Relabel to a mixed status: "Harness BUILT in M0 (window.__qa); the visual/feel loop has run from M1 (audit/ screenshots). Still pending: the §2 auto-player bot, the §3 automated fun-proxy suite, and the M3+ per-milestone sweeps." Keep §2/§3 as forward, but state §1/§4 as built.

discrepancies.issue: §3.2 Option A (lines 153-154) still presents "**Option A (zero build step):** Google's dynamic subsetting" as a live delivery option. This directly contradicts the locked decision Q52/D-013 in docs/prd.md, which explicitly kills Google dynamic-subsetting ("breaks offline + the itch relative-base"). Internal inconsistency with the resolved decision.
discrepancies.suggestedFix: Drop Option A as a live choice or annotate it as rejected per Q52 ("Google dynamic subsetting — rejected: breaks offline / itch relative-base"), leaving only the self-hosted-subset Option B path.

discrepancies.issue: §3.1 heading (line 125): "### 3.1 Font stack (DRAFT recommendation — final pick flagged for the human)". The build shipped exactly this stack as the de-facto default in styles.css (--font-head/--font-body/--font-display = Shippori Mincho B1/Shippori Mincho/Yuji Syuku), so it is no longer merely a draft "flagged for the human" — it is the adopted stack (pending only the actual self-host, Q52).
discrepancies.suggestedFix: Soften the heading to "Font stack (adopted default; self-host pending Q52)" or note inline that this stack is already wired into src/ui/styles.css.

discrepancies.issue: Open choice #3 (lines 625-626): "**Palette fine-tune:** ... Worth a quick eyeball once real screens render — trivially tunable since everything is a token." Real screens have now rendered (M1/M2; audit/ screenshots) and the palette is implemented verbatim in styles.css (e.g. --shu #D7402C, --ink #26221E). The "once real screens render" precondition is satisfied.
discrepancies.suggestedFix: Note the palette has shipped as authored (tokens in styles.css match the doc) and the "eyeball on real screens" is now actionable/done, rather than a future precondition.

discrepancies.issue: §1 (line 43): "`window.__game` exposes the live instance for deeper poking." This is not built — grep for __game across src/ and index.html returns nothing; only window.__qa exists. The doc advertises an API that the build does not provide.
discrepancies.suggestedFix: Either drop the window.__game sentence, or mark it as not-yet-implemented (the built __qa already exposes state()/set()/seed() for poking).

discrepancies.issue: §7 (line 227): "**Where the harness will live:** `src/app/` (the composition root) installs `window.__qa` under `import.meta.env.DEV` ...". Future tense ("will live") for something already built — main.ts does exactly this today. (The same sentence's "the auto-player + fun-proxy collectors live as a DEV/test module" remains genuinely unbuilt, so only the harness clause is stale.)
discrepancies.suggestedFix: Change "will live" → "lives": the __qa harness is installed in src/app/main.ts under import.meta.env.DEV. Keep the auto-player/fun-proxy collector clause as pending (still accurate).

discrepancies.issue: §4 (line 148): "(per the [UI design-language bible](../ui-design.md), authored before M1's first real UI)". Historically true, but it now reads as if M1's UI does not yet exist; M1+M2 UI is built and this very loop has already produced audit/ screenshots for it.
discrepancies.suggestedFix: Reword to present tense, e.g. "(per the UI design-language bible, which the M1/M2 renderer is built to)" — drop the pre-M1 framing.

discrepancies.issue: §5 Correctness sweep (line ~181) claims `npm run verify` includes "K/M/B + macron lints + the §4.8 pacing regression". The actual verify gate (package.json) is tsc + eslint + prettier + vitest + verify-content + gen:docs --check; scripts/verify-content.ts enforces only id/canon invariants (no macron, no KMB, no pacing checks). K/M/B is covered only by a unit test (format.test.ts); a macron lint and a §4.8 pacing regression do not exist in the gate yet.
discrepancies.suggestedFix: Align the §5 description with the real verify gate, or mark the macron lint + §4.8 pacing regression as planned additions (not yet in `npm run verify`). KMB is validated by unit tests, not a lint.

discrepancies.issue: §3 (lines 147-150): "the auto-player + `pacing()` proxies ... are the **measurement mechanism** (run on every build as a "fun regression suite")". pacing() exists, but the auto-player bot and an automated fun-regression suite are NOT built and do NOT run on every build — only a throwaway prototype (tmp/playtest.mjs) and the verify gate (which has no fun/pacing proxy) exist. The parenthetical reads as present-tense-built.
discrepancies.suggestedFix: Soften to forward tense, e.g. "(intended to run on every build as a fun regression suite once the §2 auto-player + §3 proxy suite land)"; pacing() is built but the suite is not yet wired into verify.

accurate: ui-design.md open-choice #4 (lines 627-628): "Standing & Office kanji = 官威 (kan'i) — RESOLVED 2026-06-25" is current and correct; src/ui §5.3 and src/core content use 官威 consistently.
ui-design.md palette tokens are faithfully implemented: styles.css carries --shu #D7402C, --ink #26221E, the washi/ai/beni roles etc. exactly as the doc specifies.

ui-design.md's component/motion language is BUILT: the §6.2 rank-up seal press (commit ca3eef0), the .frame/hanko seal, the four-pillar identities, the a11y win-rate fix, the Settings/About modal all appear in src/ui/render.ts.
qa-playtesting.md §1 "Built in M0 ... gated on import.meta.env.DEV, stripped from production" is accurate — main.ts:159 guards the __qa install behind import.meta.env.DEV.

qa-playtesting.md §2 (the headless auto-player bot with optimal/casual variants + telemetry + committed seeds) is CORRECTLY still future — it is genuinely NOT built; only a toRung greedy helper inside __qa and a throwaway tmp/playtest.mjs prototype exist. The future-tense framing there is accurate, not stale.
qa-playtesting.md §3 fun-proxy suite and the §4.8 pacing budget / 70-30 / >=30-min targets are correctly forward — they need T1/T2, which are not built. Not stale.

fun-factor.md is largely correct-as-intent: it describes targets for the full ~28.5h T0-T2 climb, most of which is unbuilt (only T0 partial), so its forward framing (and the "human play-judgment at M3 is the real test") is defensible, not stale.
None of the three docs carry stale NPC names — they don't reference the retired Yagyū/Edogawa echoes; the code (src/core/content/names.ts) uses Shigemasa / Kihei / Sōan.
T3/T4 and the delegation/auto-producer material in fun-factor §5/§8 are correctly marked forward/roadmap-only and match the PRD's T3+ gating.
reports.notes:
Method: read all three docs in full; verified against repo HEAD (git log shows 9 build commits incl. 8bf6ac9 M0+M1, 248bf93 M2a+b, fc36172 win-rate/combat-dead-end fix, ca3eef0 seal press); inspected src/app/main.ts (the __qa surface), src/ui/render.ts + styles.css, scripts/verify-content.ts, package.json, audit/ screenshots, tmp/*.mjs, and memory/project-status.md.

Central correction to the task framing: the §2 \"headless auto-player (the bot)\" and the §3 automated \"fun regression suite\" are NOT actually built. What exists is (a) window.__qa (built, M0), (b) a toRung greedy time-compression helper inside __qa, and (c) a throwaway Playwright prototype tmp/playtest.mjs that auto-plays to R3, auto-fights, and asserts no-stall/monotonic progress — THAT is what caught the combat dead-end (fixed in fc36172), not the §2 bot. So qa-playtesting §2/§3 future tense is genuinely accurate; the stale markers are concentrated in §1's \"PLAN/harness lands in M0\" banner and §7's \"will live\" — the parts that ARE built.

Font reality (Q52): the build ships NO self-hosted/Google font — there is no @font-face, no /public/fonts, no Google link. The CSS font stack names Shippori/Yuji but resolves to system mincho fallback (Hiragino Mincho ProN / Yu Mincho). This makes ui-design's two font open-choices the highest-value fixes: #1 (\"confirm before M1\") is overtaken (M1 shipped on the draft default + system fallback) and #2 (delivery method) is already locked to self-host by Q52/D-013. project-status confirms \"Pre-publish: self-host the OFL font (Q52)\" is the outstanding task.

Severity logic: \"major\" = a status/decision marker that materially misrepresents built reality (DRAFT-on-an-implemented-bible; a resolved decision still shown as open; a PLAN banner over a built harness). \"minor\" = localized future-tense/inconsistency that a reader would catch but isn't misleading about the project's state.

Tooling note (not a doc issue): the working tree has one uncommitted change, src/ui/styles.css (git status \" M\"); 51 unit tests confirmed across the .test.ts files. Did not run npm verify (read-only audit)."

discrepancies.issue: No living roadmap / milestone-completion tracker exists. D-021 (and the PRD framing + docs/README) mandate that, once M0+M1 are built and playtested, "§7 moves to a living docs/roadmap.md (banner: 'M0–M1 committed; M2–M7 provisional, re-planned after each playtest')". That trigger is now MET: M0+M1+M2a+M2b are built (9 build commits this session, 8bf6ac9→4f8abc2) AND a real playtest happened (journal §7 — the combat dead-end the playtest caught). Today the ONLY place tracking 'M0/M1/M2 done, M3 next' is memory/project-status.md + the journal; nothing canonical/living does. D-022's human-signed sequencing note leaves explosion timing 'TBD — can land ... after the first playtest', so this is now due/warranted, not a hard violation.
discrepancies.suggestedFix: Create docs/roadmap.md as the living milestone tracker with the mandated banner, marking M0 / M1 / M2a / M2b DONE (with commit refs), M3a NEXT, M3b–M7 provisional — and have it own milestone-completion going forward. If the human prefers to keep deferring the full docs-explosion, instead record an explicit decision (journal/ADR note) that project-status.md remains the interim tracker, and update the now-stale 'don't explode yet' language (below) to say the trigger has fired.

discrepancies.issue: §7.2 'Milestone roadmap (M0 … M7)' carries ZERO completion markers — every milestone (incl. the already-built M0, M1, M2a, M2b) is written purely in future/imperative tense ('Goal:', 'Definition of done:', 'Lands:'). E.g. 'M0 — Toolchain bootstrap …' and 'M1 — T0 Phase-1 labour spine …' read as forward work, but both are built and verify-green. A reader of the canonical spec cannot tell M0–M2b are done while M3–M7 are genuinely forward.
discrepancies.suggestedFix: Add a status overlay at the top of §7.2 (a small table: M0 ✅ done @8bf6ac9 · M1 ✅ @8bf6ac9 · M2a/M2b ✅ @248bf93 · M3a → NEXT · M3b–M7 provisional), or a one-line BUILT/✅ tag on each completed milestone heading. Better: delegate this to the new docs/roadmap.md and leave §7 as the spec.

discrepancies.issue: Top framing block (lines 41–46) is stale forward-looking instruction that already happened: "**Do NOT explode this doc yet** — **M0 + M1 build against this PRD as-is**; full sign-off and the one-time reorganisation come **after the first build-and-play cycle**, on ground that has survived contact with play. Then §1 + the locked constraints freeze as a tagged vision snapshot, §7 moves to a living `docs/roadmap.md` …". The first build-and-play cycle is complete (M0/M1/M2 built + playtested this session), so 'yet' / 'after the first build-and-play cycle' is overtaken by events.
discrepancies.suggestedFix: Update the block to record that the first build-and-play cycle is complete (M0–M2b shipped, playtested) and that the reorganisation/roadmap.md is now due — or, if deliberately still deferred, say so explicitly with the reason, rather than implying the build hasn't started.

discrepancies.issue: Line 13 repeats the same now-stale deferral: "**The docs-explosion is deferred.** … don't explode yet. Build M0+M1 against the *current* `prd.md` …, then reorganise **once**, later, on ground that has survived play. When the split happens (post-M0/M1 playtest) … the §7 milestone roadmap becomes a **living** `roadmap.md` …". M0+M1 (and M2) are built and playtested; the 'don't explode yet / when the split happens (post-M0/M1 playtest)' trigger has fired.
discrepancies.suggestedFix: Update line 13 to reflect that M0/M1/M2 are built and playtested and the split is now due (or deliberately still held, with reason). If docs/roadmap.md is created, link it here and drop the 'Likely future additions' framing for the roadmap.

discrepancies.issue: Process-gate line now contradicted by reality: line 7–8 'No code is written until §7 (the roadmap) is approved.' and line 6845 '… once approved, unlocks code (§ "How to read this document": *no code is written until §7 is approved*).' §7 was approved and ~9 commits of code (M0–M2b) now exist.
discrepancies.suggestedFix: Annotate as satisfied/historical, e.g. append '(satisfied 2026-06-26: §7 approved → M0–M2b built)', so the gate reads as a met precondition rather than a still-open prohibition.

discrepancies.issue: Version-label drift in the masthead/ToC. Line 3 says 'Status: PRD V2 — reshaped from the 79 human-signed V2 decisions (Block L Q1–Q56 + Block M FU1–FU23 …)' and the §-status table (lines 23–29) rows all read 'PRD V2 — …'. But Block N + N.1 (the V2.1/V2.2 decisions) are woven throughout the body (commit 2b8d5e9 'PRD V2.2: apply all Block N + N.1', and ~10 in-text 'Block N.1' references), and the journal/project-status call the doc 'PRD V2.2'. The masthead never names V2.1/V2.2 or Block N.
discrepancies.suggestedFix: Bump the masthead Status to 'PRD V2.2' and cite Block N + N.1 alongside Block L/M; optionally update the §-status table cells to 'PRD V2.2'.

discrepancies.issue: The live 'how to resume' snapshot points at a git-ignored throwaway. Line 31: 'Headless QA harness: `tmp/qa-shots.mjs` (Playwright) → screenshots in `audit/`.' and line 43 resume step 3: 'Drive headlessly: `node tmp/qa-shots.mjs` …'. tmp/ is git-ignored (`tmp/*` except `tmp/README.md`; `git ls-files tmp/` = only README), so tmp/qa-shots.mjs does NOT survive a fresh clone — yet it is cited as the canonical resume command. CLAUDE.md says anything worth keeping must graduate out of tmp/.
discrepancies.suggestedFix: Either graduate the headless harness to a tracked path (e.g. scripts/qa-shots.mjs) and update the references, or change the resume step to the tracked mechanisms (the capture-game-states skill and/or the window.__qa console API) and stop citing the git-ignored tmp file.

discrepancies.issue: No R-item is queued even though the demo is built and explicitly awaiting the human's fun/taste verdict: project-status.md line 45 says '**Next (post-review):** M3 …', and CLAUDE.md names the human as 'the final fun & taste arbiter'. review.md is '_None yet._', so the implied pending human review of the playable M0–M2 demo isn't surfaced in the human's queue.
discrepancies.suggestedFix: File one R-item: 'R1 — review the M0–M2 demo for fun/pacing/look (cold open → labour → wolf → combat)', with how-to-look (npm run dev or the audit/qa-*.png gallery). Then 'Next (post-review)' has something concrete to gate on.

discrepancies.file: tmp/ (merge-prd.py, v21-decisions.md, wf-prd-v22.mjs, wf-prd-v22-audit.mjs, debug-combat.ts, measure-combat.ts, *.log)
discrepancies.issue: Dead staging artifacts from the V2.1/V2.2 PRD work (now done) linger in tmp/. NB: the task's premise of 'a scratchpad/ (v21-decisions.md, merge-prd.py)' is inaccurate — there is NO project scratchpad/ directory; both files live in tmp/, which is git-ignored (only tmp/README.md is tracked), so they do not pollute the committed repo.
discrepancies.suggestedFix: Optional cleanup only: `rm` the spent tmp/ scratch (merge-prd.py, v21-decisions.md, wf-prd-v22*.mjs, *.log). No repo action is strictly required since tmp/ is git-ignored. Do NOT delete tmp/qa-shots.mjs without first relocating it (see the project-status finding).

accurate: human-in-the-loop/decisions.md is '_None yet._' — no stale/resolved H-items left queued (resolved forks correctly graduated to docs/history/decisions.md, where D-044 crash-recovery and D-045 a11y-ink are present).
human-in-the-loop/review.md is '_None yet._' — no stale R-item is falsely marked open (the gap is the inverse: a missing item, flagged as minor).
brainstorms/PARKED-THREADS.md is '_None yet._' — accurate; there are genuinely no parked threads to mark resolved/built.

docs/content/t0-content.md is current: `npx tsx scripts/gen-docs.ts --check` reports 'generated docs are up to date', and it is part of `npm run verify`. Its cast table carries the canonical post-rename names (lord Shigemasa, drillmaster Kihei, physician Sōan), matching ground truth.

journal/2026-06-26-session-02-overnight-build.md is a faithful chronological record (summary-at-top, append-at-bottom). The §3 'physician Ryōan' and Sadayori/Tsuneoka are the M0-era intermediate names truthfully captured at that point, then correctly reconciled to Shigemasa/Kihei/Sōan in §5 — correct journaling of how state evolved, not a stale error.
M3–M7 (and T3/T4) being written in future/imperative tense across PRD §7 is CORRECT — they are genuinely unbuilt and roadmap-only.
tmp/ being git-ignored except its README is consistent with CLAUDE.md's 'temporary files → ./tmp/' rule; no dead staging artifact is actually committed to the repo.
reports.notes: Scope: markdown milestone/roadmap/housekeeping audit, verified against repo state (git log/ls-files, gen:docs --check, file reads). Headline: there is NO canonical/living milestone-completion tracker — PRD §7 has zero done-markers and the D-021-mandated docs/roadmap.md does not exist, even though its trigger (M0+M1 built + playtested) is now met; project-status.md + the journal are the only de-facto trackers. The same now-stale 'don't explode yet / build M0+M1 first' instruction appears in three places (prd.md framing block, prd.md §7 header, docs/README.md) and should be reconciled together. HITL queues and PARKED-THREADS are clean. Housekeeping is mostly fine because tmp/ is git-ignored; the one real housekeeping bug is project-status.md citing a git-ignored tmp/qa-shots.mjs as the resume command. Out-of-scope observation: `git status` shows an uncommitted modification to src/ui/styles.css (not a doc issue, but the working tree is not clean)."

workflowProgress.label: prd-consistency
